title: Super Operators can arbitrarily transfer any user's tokens
reason: This is an explicit admin-controlled trust role by design: transfer authorization intentionally includes `_superOperators[msg.sender]`. It is a centralization/property-of-privileged-role concern, not a permissionless vulnerability.

title: approveFor allows setting approval on tokens user doesn't own
reason: `approveFor` requires `msg.sender` to be the `sender`, a trusted meta-tx processor, a super operator, or an already-approved operator-for-all, and separately requires `owner == sender`. The cited unauthorized path is not supported by the code.

title: setApprovalForAllFor allows super operators to self-approve
reason: Super operators already have global transfer power, so self-approval adds no new capability or exploitable permissionless path. This is redundant with the intended privileged role.

title: Missing zero-address validation when changing admin
reason: Setting admin to `address(0)` is an admin-only misconfiguration risk, not an externally exploitable protocol vulnerability. It does not create realistic permissionless fund loss by itself.

title: Land contract does not implement ERC-165 supportsInterface correctly
reason: `supportsInterface` correctly returns true for the three advertised interface IDs and false otherwise. The claim about 'multiple interface IDs' is not meaningful for ERC-165, which checks one `bytes4` at a time.

title: batchTransferFrom does not verify token existence before transfer
reason: `_batchTransferFrom` loads `(owner, operatorEnabled)` via `_ownerAndOperatorEnabledOf(id)` and requires `owner == from`, while `from` must be nonzero. Unminted tokens resolve to `owner == address(0)` and cannot pass the check.

title: No two-step process for admin transfer creates permanent lockup risk
reason: This is a governance best-practice issue only. It depends on operator mistake and does not describe a concrete exploitable protocol flaw.

title: mintQuad can overflow when calculating token count
reason: `size` is strictly constrained to `1`, `3`, `6`, `12`, or `24`, so `size * size` is at most `576`. The proposed overflow cannot occur in the reachable code path.

title: setMinter lacks zero-address validation
reason: Allowing `address(0)` in the minter mapping is benign configuration noise; it does not grant power or create an exploitable state transition.

title: MetaTransactionReceiver allows setting any address as meta transaction processor
reason: This is an admin-trusted configuration choice. Pointing the processor to an EOA may disable or centralize meta-tx handling, but it is not a standalone permissionless vulnerability.
